content.full_text:
February 15, 1980


Jesse M. Davis
5081 McCormber Rd.
Buena Park, CA 90621

Dear Mr. Davis:

In your letter of January 20, 1980, you first ask whether
you may limit your contributions to a maximum of $25 from
each contributor. The Political Reform Act, to which our
advice in this letter must be limited, contains no limitations
on the amount of contributions. A number of municipalities
and counties in California impose contribution ceilings. To
the law in each local jurisdiction should be checked to
determine if such limits exist. Generally speaking, if you
wish voluntarily to impose limits on the amounts of contribu-
tions you will receive, that is your business.

In your second question you ask whether you may operate
without a committee but still have another person as treasurer.
You are required to file campaign statements as a candidate
(see Government Code Section 84200). Nothing in the Political
Reform Act requires you to form a committee. If the person
you call your treasurer is merely your agent, and if you
can, upon signing your candidate's statement, verify that
to the best of your knowledge the statement and its attached
schedules are true, correct and complete and that you have
used all reasonable diligence in their preparation with
respect to all transactions, then you can report all trans-
actions on your candidate's statements. If, on the other
hand, with respect to some transactions you can only verify
that your treasurer has used all reasonable diligence in the
preparation of the statements and its schedules, then the
treasurer should file a statement of organization as a recipient
committee within ten days of the time the treasurer receives
$500. Generally speaking, if the treasurer has any discretion
at all in the handling of funds or if there are any transactions
of which you do not have full personal knowledge, it would
be advisable to form a committee.

Jesse M. Davis
February 15, 1980
Page Two


Your third question inquires whether the present use of
signs and buttons left over from the 1976 campaign, payments
for which were disclosed then, must be reported as either a
receipt or an expenditure in the current campaign. The
answer to the question is no. The expenditure in question
was reportable by you and reported in 1976. There is no
provision in the Political Reform Act for additional reporting
when the materials purchased are used.

I hope these remarks satisfactorily answer your questions.
If you require additional information, you may call me at
(916) 322-5602.

Sincerely,


John Giansello
Staff Services Analyst

JG:clh

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. BOX 807
SACRAMENTO CALIF.

SIRS,

I AM GOING TO RUN FOR RE-ELECTION TO THE
BERN PARK CITY COUNCIL. CAN YOU ANSWER THESE
QUESTIONS FOR ME PLEASE

1. MY BUDGET IS $2500.00. I INTEND TO
HAVE A LIMIT OF $2500 ON EACH CONTRIBUTION
IS THIS WITHIN THE LAW?

2. I DO NOT INTEND TO HAVE A COMMITTEE—
TO-ELECT BUT I WILL HAVE A TREASURER
IS THIS WITHIN THE LAW?

3. I INTEND TO USE SOME SIGNS AND BUTTONS
I HAD LEFT FROM THE CAMPAIGN IN
1976 — THEY WERE REPORTED AS EXPENSE
AT THAT TIME. SINCE THEY HAVE ALREADY
BEEN REPORTED AND PAID FOR IN 1976 DO
I HAVE TO REPORT THEM AGAIN?

THANK YOU
JIM HARRIS
5681 MACUMBER RD
BERN PARK
CALIF. 90621

BUENA PARK
CALIFORNIA

JESSE M. DAVIS
COUNCILMAN

CITY OF BUENA PARK
6650 BEACH BOULEVARD 90620
714-521-1586        714-521-9900
